pattern: Since at most one held key produced one over-long run, every possible original string differs from the typed one by shortening exactly one maximal run of repeated letters, so the count is just one plus how much slack each run has.
hints: Split the typed string into its maximal runs of identical consecutive letters; the clumsy extra key presses, if any, all happened inside a single one of those runs.

For a run of length g, Alice could have originally intended anywhere from 1 up to g copies of that letter there, but only one run in the whole string is allowed to be shortened at all -- every other run must stay exactly as typed.
Count the baseline string itself once, then add, for every run, the number of strictly shorter lengths it could have had (g - 1) -- summing those over all runs gives every distinct original string.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, several runs of varying length
statement example, no repeated letters at all
statement example, the entire string is one run
two identical characters, the smallest possible run longer than one
alternating runs of length two throughout
one long run flanked by singletons

stress.description: random run-length-encoded string built from a handful of runs with varying lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    chars = []
    prev = None
    while len(chars) < n:
        c = rng.choice('abcdefghij')
        while c == prev:
            c = rng.choice('abcdefghij')
        run_len = rng.randint(1, min(10, n - len(chars)))
        chars.extend([c] * run_len)
        prev = c
    return [''.join(chars[:n])]
